learning_outcomes.description: Critically evaluate the role of the registered nurse and in providing safe person-centred, ethical and legal care to adults experiencing acute health care needs.
Critically analyse and evaluate best practice models of care for supporting the management of individuals experiencing acute health care episodes.
Analyse how cultural diversity impacts on individuals in acute care environments

Through the development of theoretical knowledge; assess, plan, implement and evaluate a plan of care for persons experiencing acute health issues in a range of healthcare environments, using best available evidence.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

This course contains weekly online lectures, as per the UQ timetable, across the semester. These lectures will introduce new information and expand on key acute care concepts and their application to nursing practice.

It is expected that students undertaking this course engage with the weekly online lectures and complete any associated preparatory work.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Lectures This course contains weekly online lectures, as per the UQ timetable, across the semester. These lectures will introduce new information and expand on key acute care concepts and their application to nursing practice. It is expected that students undertaking this course engage with the weekly online lectures and complete any associated preparatory work.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Additional Online Tutorials

This course contains fortnightly online tutorials, as per the UQ timetable, across the semester. These tutorials will provide an opportunity to explore key acute care concepts in greater depth and support students to apply these concepts to nursing practice.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 13(03 Aug - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Inquiry Based Learning tutorials

This course contains five inquiry-based learning (IBL) tutorials as per the UQ timetable, over the course of the semester to assist in consolidating knowledge and developing critical thinking skills.

It is an expectation that students undertaking this course complete preparatory work and attend IBLs.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
